message:
feat(runtime): add periodic health snapshots and expose via IPC status endpoint

runtime.rs:
- RuntimeConfig gains pane_priorities and capture_budgets fields,
  plumbed through to HotReloadableConfig.
- Maintenance task now computes a HealthSnapshot every 30 seconds,
  gathering: observed pane count, capture/write queue depths, per-pane
  last_seq cursors, ingest lag (avg/max), DB writability, and
  backpressure warnings when queues exceed 75% capacity.
- HealthSnapshot is stored globally via HealthSnapshot::update_global.

ipc.rs:
- The Status handler now includes the global HealthSnapshot in its
  response payload, giving IPC clients access to runtime health data.

main.rs (wa CLI):
- run_watcher passes priorities and budgets from config to RuntimeConfig.
- wa status --health now queries the watcher via IPC first, falling
  back to the in-process global snapshot only if IPC is unavailable.
- wa status (non-health) also fetches watcher health via IPC for the
  health snapshot appended to pane output.
- Adds watcher_running, watcher, and watcher_error fields to the
  health JSON output for clearer diagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
